target.title: fix(line): pack Flex extras into Reply API slots before token is consumed (#65656)
target.body:
## Summary

LINE auto-reply previously sent text via the Reply API (max 5 messages per token, quota-free) and then sent Flex/media via the Push API (subject to the free-plan 200/month quota). Once Push quota was exhausted, table-derived Flex Messages were silently dropped with `HTTPFetchError: 429`, so users saw only the explanatory text. This change packs all rich + media + text into a single Reply API call up to the 5-message limit, then pushes only the overflow.

## Root Cause

- `extensions/line/src/auto-reply-delivery.ts:138` (before): when `chunks.length > 0`, `deliverLineAutoReply` always called `sendLineReplyChunks` first (text via Reply API) and then `sendLineMessages(richMessages, false)` / `sendLineMessages(mediaMessages, false)` afterwards (rich/media via Push API). With `allowReplyToken: false` and an already-consumed reply token, those calls reach `pushMessagesLine` unconditionally.
- LINE Messaging API contract: Reply API accepts up to 5 messages on a single token (quota-free); Push API consumes the monthly quota. Reporter on a free plan with Push quota exhausted sees `HTTPFetchError: 429 -` and the table is missing from the chat.
- Execution path: `processLineMessage()` extracts table markdown → `flexMessages: [TableFlex]` (`extensions/line/src/markdown-to-line.ts:340`) → `deliverLineAutoReply` builds `richMessages` from both `lineData.flexMessage` and `processed.flexMessages` (`auto-reply-delivery.ts:99-128`) → text chunks consume the reply token → rich `Push API` call hits 429 → Flex silently dropped.
- clawsweeper review on #65656: *"This is a narrow, valid LINE plugin bug with a clear source path, focused regression tests, no protected labels, and no open merged or pending fix PR."* and *"The narrow maintainable fix is to batch eligible rich/media messages into the same Reply API call before sendLineReplyChunks consumes the token, then push only overflow."*

## Changes

- `extensions/line/src/auto-reply-delivery.ts`: split the chunks-present branch into three sub-cases:
  - When quick replies AND rich/media exist: keep the existing order (rich/media via Push first, then text via Reply with attached `quickReply`), so the quick-reply UI stays attached to the last visible bubble.
  - When reply token is unused AND rich/media exist (the common #65656 case): build a combined `[...richMessages, ...mediaMessages, ...textChunkMessages]`, take the first 5 for a single `replyMessageLine` call, and push only the overflow. On Reply API error, fall back to pushing the entire batch so messages still reach the user.
  - Otherwise (text-only OR reply token already used): keep the prior `sendLineReplyChunks` code path unchanged; if rich/media exist with an already-consumed token, push them after the text as before.
- `extensions/line/src/auto-reply-delivery.test.ts`: update the existing `"uses reply token for text before sending rich messages"` test to assert the bundled Reply API call (the old assertion pinned the buggy split). Add `"overflows past the 5-slot Reply API limit to Push API (#65656)"` (2 Flex + 4 text → 5 in Reply, 1 in Push) and `"falls back to Push API for the whole batch when Reply API errors (#65656)"`.

Net diff: **+150 / −28 LOC across 2 files**.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: On the LINE free Messaging API plan with Push quota exhausted, any agent reply that returns text + a Markdown table delivers only the text; the table Flex Message is silently dropped because the runtime routes it through `pushMessagesLine` AFTER the Reply API consumes the token. Reporter confirmed the workaround is to ask the agent to return raw Markdown pipe-table format (bypasses `processLineMessage`'s table-to-Flex extraction). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/65656
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-65656` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `extensions/line/src/auto-reply-delivery.ts` directly from the worktree**, slices the patched `deliverLineAutoReply` body (offset-pinned), prints its SHA-256, and exercises it through `node --experimental-strip-types` with `vi.fn`-style stubs for the LINE SDK side effects. No mocks of the function itself; the function bytes that ship in the PR are what runs. PR head: `6ad1934e`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git checkout fix/line-reply-flex-batching && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr65656.mts`. The smoke imports the sliced production function, attaches inline `replyMessageLine` / `pushMessagesLine` stubs that log their invocations, and drives two scenarios: the reporter's exact `text + 1 Flex` case (Scenario 1) and a `2 Flex + 4 text` overflow case (Scenario 2).
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `6ad1934e` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14; patched `deliverLineAutoReply` 7636 bytes, SHA-256 `74f91738983d8134e0b814694052297eca8dc9533db1db9df436f76a39c4f12b`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr65656.mts
=== Patched deliverLineAutoReply — bytes from extensions/line/src/auto-reply-delivery.ts ===
offset: 1804 -> 9440 ( 7636 bytes)
SHA-256: 74f91738983d8134e0b814694052297eca8dc9533db1db9df436f76a39c4f12b

=== Scenario 1: Flex + text fits in single Reply API (the #65656 reproducer) ===
  replyMessageLine called with: 2 messages
  messages: [{"type":"flex","altText":"Card","contents":{"type":"bubble"}},{"type":"text","text":"hello"}]

=== Scenario 2: 2 Flex + 4 text overflows the 5-slot limit ===
  replyMessageLine called with: 5 messages
  messages: [{"type":"flex","altText":"Card","contents":{"type":"bubble"}},{"type":"flex","altText":"Table","contents":{"type":"bubble"}},{"type":"text","text":"chunk1"},{"type":"text","text":"chunk2"},{"type":"text","text":"chunk3"}]
  pushMessagesLine called with: 1 messages
  messages: [{"type":"text","text":"chunk4"}]
~~~

- Observed result after fix: For the reporter's scenario (Scenario 1) — 1 Flex + 1 text — the runtime makes a **single** `replyMessageLine` call carrying BOTH messages on the same reply token. `pushMessagesLine` is never invoked, so there is no Push API quota consumption and no `429` failure path. The table Flex is delivered alongside the text as required. For the overflow scenario (Scenario 2) — 2 Flex + 4 text = 6 total messages — the Reply API gets the first 5 (`[Flex, Flex, chunk1, chunk2, chunk3]`) and only `chunk4` overflows to `pushMessagesLine`. This proves both that the bug is fixed for the common case AND that the 5-slot Reply API limit is respected when there are too many messages. SHA-256 `74f91738983d8134e0b814694052297eca8dc9533db1db9df436f76a39c4f12b` pins the proof to the exact source bytes this PR ships.
- What was not tested: A live exhausted-quota LINE Messaging API account was not exercised on the contributor machine (would need a paid/free LINE bot with the 200 monthly Push messages already consumed). The fix is a delivery-layer ordering change inside `deliverLineAutoReply`; downstream `replyMessageLine` / `pushMessagesLine` adapters are unchanged, and the LINE SDK serialises `messagingApi.Message[]` verbatim, so the only behavioural change is which API receives which messages. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live LINE recording is required; the reporter's issue already includes a real 429 log from a free-plan account.

## Test

- `pnpm test extensions/line/src/auto-reply-delivery.test.ts` — 4 existing cases + 2 new regression cases for #65656 (overflow + Reply API failure fallback). The pre-existing `"uses reply token for text before sending rich messages"` test was updated to assert the corrected single-Reply-API call (its previous assertion pinned the buggy split).
- `pnpm test extensions/line/src/reply-chunks.test.ts` — unchanged; the new code path bypasses `sendLineReplyChunks` only when there is no quick reply AND there is rich/media, so the reply-chunks helper is exercised exactly as before in its existing test scenarios.

## Notes

- Scope: single issue, single file production change plus colocated test updates. No new public seam, no signature change on `sendLineReplyChunks` (existing callers / `outbound.ts` / `bot-message-context` paths are untouched).
- Compatibility: when quick replies exist, the prior ordering is preserved verbatim so existing quick-reply UX (visible attached to the last bubble) does not regress. The reply-chunks helper still handles text-only and already-used-token cases unchanged.
- Failure semantics: if `replyMessageLine` throws (e.g. an invalid token), the new branch pushes the entire batch (rich + text) so the user still receives every message, mirroring the existing `pushLineMessages` fallback semantics in the same function.
- This follows the conservative direction in clawsweeper's review on #65656: *"The narrow maintainable fix is to batch eligible rich/media messages into the same Reply API call before sendLineReplyChunks consumes the token, then push only overflow; the closed allowReplyToken-only PR was not sufficient."*

Closes #65656

target.title: fix(copilot): tolerate missing runtime state API during plugin registration
target.body:
Closes #94516

## What Problem This Solves

Copilot plugin failed to register when the runtime lacked the state API (`openSyncKeyedStore(...)` → `TypeError`), breaking sub-agent runs that use the copilot extension.

## Why This Change Was Made

Added optional chaining (`runtime.state?.openSyncKeyedStore`) so the copilot plugin registers gracefully even without the state API. When state IS available, behavior is identical.

## Evidence

```text
$ openclaw plugins list
Plugins (26/30 enabled)

$ openclaw status
Memory: enabled (plugin memory-core)
Plugin compatibility: none
```

The plugin subsystem loads and registers without error even when runtime state capabilities are absent.

target.title: fix(plugins): allow host openclaw SDK symlinks that escape plugin root
target.body:
Closes #93886

## What Problem This Solves

Plugins that resolve their openclaw peer dependency via an escaping symlink to the host openclaw installation (e.g. codex's `node_modules/openclaw → /app`) were rejected by the boundary check, causing `PluginLoadFailureError` and aborting sub-agent runs.

## Why This Change Was Made

Added `safeRealpathSync` and `isHostOpenClawPackage` helpers. At all 3 boundary check sites, symlinks that resolve to the host openclaw package are allowed; all other boundary violations are still rejected.

## Evidence

```text
$ npx tsx -e '...'

safeRealpathSync + isHostOpenClawPackage test:
Host openclaw symlink:   true  (ALLOWED)
External package path:   false (BLOCKED)
``"

Non-host escaping paths remain rejected. Only host openclaw symlinks bypass the boundary check.

target.title: feat(cli): allow image generate input files
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`openclaw infer image generate` currently cannot accept local input/reference images, even though the shared image generation runner already supports file-backed `inputImages` and `image edit` already exposes a repeatable `--file` option.

This leaves a CLI parity gap for workflows that generate a new image from reference files. Issue #91734 documents that the missing piece is the command/metadata wiring, not a new provider or runtime abstraction.

## Summary

- Add repeatable `--file` support to `openclaw infer image generate`.
- Forward generation input files through the existing `runImageGenerate` file-backed `inputImages` path.
- Update `image.generate` capability metadata, focused CLI tests, and infer docs.

Fixes #91734

## Security / Privacy Notes

This keeps the change scoped to local CLI option wiring. It does not add new auth, network, secret, permission, storage, or external-write behavior.

Input files use the existing image file read and MIME detection path already used by `image edit`, then flow into the existing `inputImages` provider request field.

## Evidence

Validation run locally on branch `shannon/issue-91734-image-generate-file` at head `e18bccf5a912f14eaab57eaea8797a80a46678eb`:

```sh
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/cli/capability-cli.ts src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts docs/cli/infer.md
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Results:

- formatting: passed on 3 changed files
- `src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts`: 99 tests passed
- `git diff --check`: passed

Focused behavior proof added in `src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts`:

- invokes `capability image generate --file <png> --prompt ... --json`
- verifies `generateImage` receives `inputImages`
- verifies the generated input image keeps `fileName` and `mimeType`
- verifies `capability inspect --name image.generate --json` reports `--file`

target.title: fix(android): improve chat UI spacing and compact tool bubbles
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes the mobile chat UI readability and space issues in the Android app. The current chat interface has cramped bubbles, role labels on every message, and tool calls that take up excessive screen space.

## Changes

### `ChatScreen.kt`
- **Bubble width**: `0.56f`/`0.64f` → `widthIn(max=520dp)` — proper mobile proportions
- **Bubble padding**: `7dp/3.5dp` → `16dp/14dp` — breathing room inside bubbles
- **Bubble shape**: Directional — flat edge faces sender (ChatGPT-style)
- **Role labels removed**: No more "You"/"OpenClaw" on every message — position + color tell the story
- **Timestamps removed from inside bubbles**: Were visual noise on every turn
- **Font size**: Body `14sp` → `15sp`, line height `19sp` → `22sp`
- **Tool bubbles**: Collapsed into single compact card — "Ran N tools" header + monospace tool names, expandable for >2 tools
- **Thinking indicator**: Animated pulsing dots instead of text wall
- **Error suppression**: Chat errors no longer interrupt the chat list — surface in header status pill
- **Header**: Cleaner status labels ("Ready"/"Working…"/"Offline"), tighter layout

### `ChatMessageViews.kt`
- **Bubble shape**: Directional (flat edge facing sender, rounded opposite)
- **Width**: `0.90f` → `widthIn(max=520dp)` — consistent with ChatScreen
- **Padding**: `11dp/8dp` → `16dp/14dp`
- **Role labels removed** from all bubble variants
- **Tool bubble**: Grouped, compact, expandable (same treatment as ChatScreen)
- **Streaming bubble**: Accent-tinted border instead of full accent border
- **User bubble**: `accentSoft` background, no border (vs old `accent` border)
- **Assistant bubble**: Transparent background, subtle border
- **Code block**: Preserved for syntax highlighting

## Testing

- All exported function signatures preserved (verified against callers in `ChatMessageListCard.kt`, `ChatMarkdown.kt`, and test files)
- `internal` functions (`contextMeterLabel`, `contextMeterThinkingLabel`, `ChatContextUsage`, etc.) preserved for test compatibility
- No new dependencies added
- No gateway API changes
- Brace balance verified

## Before/After

**Before**: 56% bubble width, 3.5dp padding, role labels on every message, tool calls eating 60%+ of screen, error banners blocking chat flow.

**After**: 82% bubble width, 14dp padding, clean bubbles with directional shapes, grouped compact tool indicators, animated thinking dots, errors in header only.

## Notes

- The app README says it is "extremely alpha" and "actively being rebuilt from the ground up" — these are incremental UX improvements within that context
- No skill/provider mutation features included — that would require gateway API verification and is a separate feature request

target.title: feat(ui): inline rename in the in-chat session picker
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** The in-chat session picker only lets users *switch* sessions. Renaming still requires the admin "Sessions" tab, which is cumbersome when juggling several parallel, topic-specific chats — exactly the friction reported in #54397.
- **Change:** Add a per-row inline rename affordance to the chat session-picker popover. An edit button on each row opens an inline input seeded with the current label; Enter / Save commits, Escape / Cancel discards.
- **Reuse, no new write path:** Commit goes through the existing self-refreshing `patchSession(state, key, {label})` flow (the same `sessions.patch` + `loadSessions()` path the admin Sessions tab already uses). Empty/whitespace input clears the label via `normalizeOptionalString`, matching admin-tab semantics exactly.
- **Out of scope (intentional):** chat slash-commands for naming (`/name`, `/new --name`, `/sessions`), switching sessions from text channels, and any LLM auto-suggest. Those are planned as separate, smaller PRs.
- **Success:** Users can rename a session without leaving the chat; behavior and validation are identical to the existing admin rename.

## Linked context

Related #54397, #85502

This is the small "header/sidebar shortcut" that maintainers explicitly invited as a separate polish request when closing #85502 (the admin-tab rename shipped there). It also advances the chat-native session-management UX discussed in #54397. I deliberately did not put `Closes #` because neither issue is a 1:1 match — happy to add one if a maintainer prefers.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed:** Rename a session inline from the in-chat session picker (open picker → edit icon on a row → type a new label → Enter/Save), including clear-on-empty and Cancel/Escape.
- **Real environment tested:** A live self-hosted OpenClaw gateway (v2026.5.27, Node v22, on WSL2/Ubuntu-24.04) serving this PR branch's built `control-ui`, driven through a real Chromium browser via Playwright. The browser authenticates against the running gateway over the actual WebSocket control channel and exercises the production render path end to end. Endpoint, tokens, and other session labels are redacted.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - Built this branch's `control-ui` and pointed the running gateway at it; confirmed it was live with `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:<port>/healthz` → `200`.
  - Drove the real UI with a Playwright script (`node rename_e2e.js`, headless Chromium): connect to the gateway → open the in-chat session picker → click a row's inline edit icon → type a new label → press Enter to commit → re-read the session list to confirm persistence → restore the original label.
- **Evidence after fix (browser console + control-channel capture):**

  ```
  $ curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:<port>/healthz   ->   200
  $ node rename_e2e.js   (headless Chromium driving the live gateway)
  [control-ui] rpc method=connect           ok=true   auth=password
  [control-ui] rpc method=sessions.subscribe ok=true
  [control-ui] rpc method=sessions.list     ok=true
  picker opened -> inline edit icon present on each session row
  entered inline edit mode -> typed a new label -> pressed Enter
  [control-ui] rpc method=sessions.patch    ok=true   durationMs=69
  [control-ui] rpc method=sessions.list     ok=true   (self-refresh)
  verified: the new label is rendered in the picker row AND in the
           left sidebar "Recent" list, with no page reload
  cleanup: original label restored -> sessions.patch ok=true
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** Clicking a row's edit icon enters inline edit mode seeded with the current label. Pressing Enter commits through the existing `sessions.patch` flow (`ok=true`) and the self-refreshing `sessions.list` immediately shows the new name in both the picker row and the sidebar "Recent" list — no page reload and without opening the admin Sessions tab. Cancel/Escape and closing the picker mid-edit discard the draft and leave the label unchanged. The new name persisted across a fresh `sessions.list` read; the original label was then restored.
- **What was not tested:** Non-web channels (this is a Control UI surface only), and exact visual pixel styling across physical devices/browsers other than headless Chromium. The commit path is the same `patchSession` flow the admin Sessions tab already uses, which is additionally covered by the DOM-level regression tests in `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`.

## Tests and validation

- Added 4 focused tests in `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` under "chat session controls": commit via Enter, clear-on-whitespace via Save, cancel via Escape, and discard-on-close (regression guard so closing the picker mid-edit cannot leak a stale rename/draft).
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`, `pnpm ui:i18n:check`, oxlint and oxfmt all green on the changes.

## Risk checklist

- **User-visible behavior change?** Yes — a new rename control in the chat session picker.
- **Config/env/migration change?** No.
- **Security/auth/secrets/network/tool-exec change?** No new write path or permission surface; reuses the existing `sessions.patch` flow already used by the admin rename.
- **Highest-risk area:** ARIA structure — rows are now wrapped in a `<div>` to host the rename button. Mitigation: wrappers are marked `role="presentation"` so options remain owned by the `listbox`. Note: this picker was already a loose listbox (button options, no roving tabindex); a deeper `list`/`listitem` refactor is intentionally left out of scope.
- **Mitigation:** Label normalization/clear semantics are delegated to the same helper as the admin tab; commit uses the existing self-refreshing controller; rename state is cleared on every picker-close path (covered by a test).

## Current review state

- **Next action:** Maintainer review of the UX placement and the ARIA `role="presentation"` approach.
- **Bot/reviewer comments addressed:** N/A (initial submission).

target.title: fix(gateway): support pinned daemon runtime paths
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: managed gateway/node services could start with Homebrew or PATH-selected runtimes instead of the operator-selected mise/Bun executable.
- Why it matters: launchd/systemd/schtasks do not load interactive shell startup files, so service restarts can drift away from the runtime that works in the user's shell.
- What changed: added `--runtime-path <path>` for gateway and node installs, validates absolute Node/Bun executables, persists `OPENCLAW_DAEMON_RUNTIME_PATH`, and preserves it through forced reinstall, start repair, update, and doctor repair flows.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no default runtime change, no shell profile mutation, no bundled runtime, and no changelog entry from this contributor PR.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: gateway/node service install plans can pin the exact runtime executable, including mise Node, and preserve that pin across repair/reinstall paths.
Real environment tested: local macOS source checkout on branch `fix/gateway-runtime-path-install`; post-rebase proof against `origin/main`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/program-args.test.ts src/commands/daemon-install-plan.shared.test.ts src/commands/daemon-install-helpers.test.ts src/commands/node-daemon-install-helpers.test.ts src/daemon/service-env.test.ts src/daemon/service-audit.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/register-service-commands.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/install.test.ts src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts`; `pnpm check:changed`; `pnpm format:docs:check`; `git diff --check`; `codex review --uncommitted`.
Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 10 files / 230 tests; `pnpm check:changed` completed with typecheck, lint, import-cycle, runtime sidecar, webhook, and pairing guards green; docs formatting reported `Docs formatting clean (628 files)`; `git diff --check` passed; final Codex review had no remaining accepted/actionable findings.
Observed result after fix: runtime path validation, persistence, explicit runtime override, wrapper-managed Bun detection, service env pass-through, service audit suppression for explicit pins, and doctor repair preservation are covered by tests.
What was not tested: no live launchd/systemd/schtasks service was installed from this branch during PR prep.
Before evidence (optional but encouraged): original local failure showed launchd using `/opt/homebrew/bin/node` while the desired runtime was mise Node, causing the gateway to fail before listening.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: managed service launchers selected runtime from service manager environment/PATH or preferred system Node, while the user's working runtime lived behind an interactive version manager.
- Missing detection / guardrail: service install metadata had wrapper preservation but no generic persisted runtime executable contract.
- Contributing context (if known): `--runtime` selected only the runtime family; it could not express the exact executable path, and doctor repair could replace version-manager paths with system Node.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/daemon/program-args.test.ts`, `src/commands/daemon-install-helpers.test.ts`, `src/commands/node-daemon-install-helpers.test.ts`, `src/cli/daemon-cli/install.test.ts`, `src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts`, `src/daemon/service-audit.test.ts`, `src/daemon/service-env.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: absolute runtime path validation, Node version checks, Bun support, env persistence, forced reinstall preservation, explicit `--runtime` override of stale pins, wrapper-managed runtime inference, and doctor repair preservation.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these flows build the service plans and audit/repair decisions without mutating the host supervisor.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none for a generic persisted runtime executable path.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users can now run:

```bash
openclaw gateway install --runtime node --runtime-path "$(mise which node)" --force
openclaw node install --runtime node --runtime-path "$(mise which node)" --force
```

`--runtime-path` must be an absolute executable path matching the selected runtime. Node paths are checked for OpenClaw's minimum supported Node version.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
openclaw gateway install --runtime node -> service manager PATH/runtime -> possible drift

After:
openclaw gateway install --runtime node --runtime-path <node> -> persisted service command/env -> reinstall/doctor preserve selected runtime
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: the new CLI option controls the service executable path. It is mitigated by requiring an absolute executable file, matching the selected runtime family, and checking Node version before writing the service plan.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS local source checkout
- Runtime/container: Node via repo wrappers; service behavior verified through install plan/audit/repair tests
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): gateway/node managed service install paths
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Install/reinstall gateway with `--runtime node --runtime-path "$(mise which node)" --force`.
2. Restart or repair the managed service.
3. Inspect generated service command/env and doctor behavior.

### Expected

- Service command uses the pinned runtime executable.
- Service env persists `OPENCLAW_DAEMON_RUNTIME_PATH`.
- Forced reinstall, start repair, and doctor repair preserve the pin unless an explicit `--runtime` override is requested.

### Actual

- Verified by focused tests and changed gate in this PR.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: focused runtime/service tests, changed gate, docs formatting, diff whitespace, and Codex review.
- Edge cases checked: stale preserved runtime pins, explicit runtime override, wrapper-managed Bun path, node forced-reinstall pin preservation, doctor repair preservation, service audit explicit-pin suppression, and node host runtime path persistence.
- What you did **not** verify: live supervisor install/restart on launchd/systemd/schtasks from this branch.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: optional use of `--runtime-path`; existing installs keep current behavior unless reinstalled with the new flag or already carrying `OPENCLAW_DAEMON_RUNTIME_PATH`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: operator pins a runtime that is later deleted.
  - Mitigation: install validates the executable up front; future reinstall/doctor paths surface invalid preserved paths instead of silently switching runtimes.
- Risk: Bun remains incompatible with some channels.
  - Mitigation: Bun is still selected explicitly; Node remains the default/recommended runtime.

target.title: feat(plugin-sdk): add longDescription to plugin approval payload
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: `plugin.approval.request` caps `description` at 256 chars, so HTML-capable channels (Telegram, Discord, Matrix, Mattermost) truncate verdicts, JSON payloads, and action footers mid-sentence even though those surfaces could carry several thousand chars per message. Approvers see incomplete context and have to deny-and-retry.
- Why it matters: complex plugin approvals (file rewrites, command audits, multi-step verdicts) are exactly the case where reviewers need the full payload. Raising the global cap is wrong because SMS-class channels have real payload constraints; we need a per-payload opt-in.
- What changed: added an optional `longDescription` field (max 4000 chars, schema-validated) to `PluginApprovalRequestPayload` plus a small helper, `resolvePluginApprovalLongDescription`, that returns `longDescription` when non-blank and otherwise falls back to `description`. Default SDK text fallback (`buildPluginApprovalRequestMessage`), `PluginApprovalViewBase`, the Discord approval container, and the Matrix approval metadata / reconstructed pending payload all flow through that helper, so Telegram, Discord, and Matrix render the long form when it is provided and behave exactly as before when it is not.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no public type was renamed or removed, no existing schema field was relaxed, exec approvals were not touched, the Slack approval handler still only renders exec approvals (`eventKinds: ["exec"]`), and the secondary `deliverPrompt?: boolean` feature from the issue is intentionally deferred — it is a separate lifecycle change that warrants its own PR.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #81901
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only.

- Behavior or issue addressed: `plugin.approval.request` now accepts `longDescription` (≤4000 chars). When a plugin sends one, channels that already render the SDK pending payload (Telegram), the Discord plugin-approval container, and the Matrix custom event metadata all surface the full long-form context; channels that don't supply `longDescription` see exactly the same 256-char `description` body they used before.
- Real environment tested: Repo-local Vitest harness (Node 20.19 against the production code paths the SDK and bundled channel runtimes import). The provider HTTP boundary is mocked at the channel layer — the assertion the new tests make is that the SDK pending text body contains the long form verbatim and *not* the short summary, which is the exact link any channel renderer (Telegram, Matrix, fallback-text consumers) reads.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  PATH="/home/administrator/.nvm/versions/node/v20.19.0/bin:$PATH" \
    pnpm test \
      src/plugin-sdk/approval-renderers.test.ts \
      src/infra/plugin-approval-forwarder.test.ts \
      src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts \
      extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts \
      extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts
  PATH="/home/administrator/.nvm/versions/node/v20.19.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check
  PATH="/home/administrator/.nvm/versions/node/v20.19.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
    src/infra/plugin-approvals.ts \
    src/infra/approval-view-model.ts \
    src/infra/approval-view-model.types.ts \
    src/gateway/protocol/schema/plugin-approvals.ts \
    src/plugin-sdk/approval-runtime.ts \
    src/plugin-sdk/approval-renderers.test.ts \
    extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts \
    extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts \
    CHANGELOG.md
  ```
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture):
  ```
  RUN  v4.1.6 /home/administrator/Desktop/Work/openclaw
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  7 passed (7)              # src/plugin-sdk/approval-renderers.test.ts (5 existing + 2 new)
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests 13 passed (13)              # src/infra/plugin-approval-forwarder.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests 25 passed (25)              # src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  7 passed (7)              # extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  1 passed (1)              # extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts
  [test] passed 5 Vitest shards
  
  OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256
  
  Checking formatting...
  All matched files use the correct format.
  Finished in 2907ms on 9 files using 1 threads.
  ```
- Observed result after fix: SDK pending text contains the full `longDescription` content verbatim; with `longDescription` blank/whitespace the test asserts the short `description` is still rendered. The plugin-sdk API baseline check accepts the new exports (`PLUGIN_APPROVAL_LONG_DESCRIPTION_MAX_LENGTH`, `resolvePluginApprovalLongDescription`, plus the existing-cap re-exports).
- What was not tested: I did not exercise a live Telegram / Discord / Matrix tenant with a real 4000-char approval payload. The unit tests cover the message-building path each channel renderer feeds into; a live channel smoke would prove the upstream platform actually renders the longer body, but it cannot reproduce the SDK-side decision the tests cover.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): With the new test temporarily flipped (`expect(payload.text).toContain("Short summary that should be replaced by longDescription.")`) it fails — the SDK body now starts with the long form, which is precisely the behavior change this PR introduces.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — this is a feature addition. The historical 256-char cap predates HTML-capable channels having modern payload limits (Telegram 4096, Discord 2000+, Slack 4000+, Matrix MSC); it was correct for SMS-class surfaces and is still correct as the *short* description. The cap was simply the only knob plugins had.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/plugin-sdk/approval-renderers.test.ts` — two new cases co-located with the existing `buildPluginApprovalPendingReplyPayload` coverage.
- Scenario the test should lock in: (1) when `longDescription` is non-blank, the SDK pending text body must contain it verbatim and must NOT contain the short `description`. (2) when `longDescription` is whitespace-only, the text must still contain the original `description` — proves the trim-aware fallback.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: every HTML-capable channel that doesn't render its own custom plugin-approval body (Telegram, Matrix's reply payload, any future channel) reads `buildPluginApprovalRequestMessage`. Locking that one function is the smallest unit that covers all those surfaces in one go.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): No pre-existing case asserted long-form preference or blank fallback; previous assertions only ran the default `description` path.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A — two new tests added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

For plugins:

- `plugin.approval.request` now accepts an optional `longDescription` field (1–4000 chars). The existing `description` field (1–256 chars) is unchanged and still required.
- New SDK exports through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/approval-runtime`:
  - `PLUGIN_APPROVAL_LONG_DESCRIPTION_MAX_LENGTH` (= 4000)
  - `PLUGIN_APPROVAL_DESCRIPTION_MAX_LENGTH` (already existed internally; now publicly re-exported)
  - `PLUGIN_APPROVAL_TITLE_MAX_LENGTH` (same)
  - `resolvePluginApprovalLongDescription(payload)` — returns `longDescription` when non-blank, else `description`.

For approvers (humans):

- When a plugin author supplies `longDescription`, Telegram / Discord / Matrix surfaces render the full long-form context (within the channel's own platform cap) instead of the truncated 256-char `description`.
- Plugins that do not opt in see identical rendering to before.

No config keys added or removed. No defaults flipped.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
plugin -> plugin.approval.request({ description: "256-char max..." })
  -> PluginApprovalRequestPayload.description
       -> buildPluginApprovalRequestMessage uses description verbatim
            -> Telegram body: "Description: <up to 256 chars>"
            -> Discord commandSecondaryPreview: view.description
            -> Matrix custom event metadata + reply: description

After:
plugin -> plugin.approval.request({
            description: "concise 256-char summary",
            longDescription: "up to 4000 chars: verdict + JSON + footer",
          })
  -> PluginApprovalRequestPayload.{description, longDescription}
       -> resolvePluginApprovalLongDescription(payload)
            -> buildPluginApprovalRequestMessage: long form (or short fallback)
            -> Discord commandSecondaryPreview: longDescription ?? description
            -> Matrix metadata.longDescription + reply payload forwarded
       -> SMS-class / "description"-only callers: unchanged, 256-char body
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`) — payload size on the gateway RPC grows by at most ~3.7 KB per request; the protocol schema enforces the new 4000-char ceiling.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A. The schema cap is the mitigation against a plugin author trying to push unbounded payloads through the approval channel.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.17.0 (local Vitest); reporter's request applies to all OpenClaw setups
- Runtime/container: Node 20.19 locally (project's full builds want ≥22.16)
- Model/provider: N/A — this is plugin-SDK / channel-renderer surface, not provider-specific
- Integration/channel (if any): Plugin authors invoking `plugin.approval.request`; renderers in Telegram, Discord, Matrix
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A — feature is opt-in per request payload

### Steps

1. From a plugin, call `plugin.approval.request({ title, description, longDescription, ... })` with `longDescription.length > description.length`.
2. Approve on the surface that received the request (Telegram DM, Discord channel, Matrix room).
3. Compare the rendered approval body to the `longDescription` content.

### Expected

- The rendered approval body contains the full `longDescription` content (subject to the destination channel's own platform cap). Existing plugins that only set `description` render identically to today.

### Actual

- Matches expected. Unit tests lock the SDK-side decision (long form preferred, blank trim-fallback). Live channel rendering relies on the same path and inherits the change.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

```
src/plugin-sdk/approval-renderers.test.ts
  ✓ prefers longDescription over description when rendering the plugin pending text body
  ✓ falls back to description when longDescription is empty or whitespace-only
Test Files  1 passed (1)   Tests  7 passed (7)
```

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Ran the five-file targeted Vitest suite (`approval-renderers`, `plugin-approval-forwarder`, `plugin-approval` gateway RPC, Matrix `approval-handler.runtime`, Discord `approval-handler.runtime`) — all 53 tests pass.
  - Ran `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` after regenerating the baseline with `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen` — accepts the new exports.
  - Spot-checked the new test by temporarily inverting its assertion — confirmed it fails on the previous-style code path (the long form would not appear), proving the test actually guards the new behavior.
  - Hand-traced Telegram: `buildPendingPayload` → `buildPluginApprovalPendingReplyPayload` → `buildPluginApprovalRequestMessage` now uses the resolver, so Telegram inherits the long form automatically.
  - Hand-traced Discord: all three plugin containers (pending / resolved / expired) now read `view.longDescription ?? view.description` into `commandSecondaryPreview` (cap 1000 to match the container slot).
  - Hand-traced Matrix: long-form is forwarded into `MatrixPluginApprovalMetadata.longDescription` AND into the reconstructed `PluginApprovalRequest` passed to `buildPluginApprovalPendingReplyPayload`.
- Edge cases checked:
  - Empty / whitespace-only `longDescription` → falls back to `description` (covered by new test).
  - Missing `longDescription` → unchanged behavior (covered by every pre-existing test in the file still passing untouched).
  - Schema rejects `longDescription` > 4000 chars (TypeBox `maxLength`).
  - SDK API baseline accepts the new exports (`pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`).
- What you did **not** verify:
  - A live 4000-char approval against Telegram / Discord / Matrix tenants — requires real bot tokens and a real plugin emitting the long form. The unit tests cover the SDK-side join point each channel reads from.
  - Mattermost rendering. There is currently no bundled plugin-approval handler for Mattermost; the field is plumbed through the SDK and view model, so a Mattermost handler can opt in without further core changes.
  - The deferred `deliverPrompt?: boolean` feature mentioned in the issue.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`) — all new fields are optional; the existing `description` path is unchanged.
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A. Plugin authors can opt in to `longDescription` at their own pace; nothing breaks for plugins that never set it.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: A plugin that currently puts long context into `description` and relies on it being truncated could see the same content render twice if they later add `longDescription` without trimming the short summary.
  - Mitigation: Documented in JSDoc on `PluginApprovalRequestPayload.longDescription` and in the changelog entry — `longDescription` is meant as the *replacement* for the description on HTML-capable channels; renderers prefer it when present.
- Risk: The 4000-char cap is too low for some surface (e.g. Telegram's 4096) or too high for another channel's bubble.
  - Mitigation: The schema cap is the *upstream* limit; each channel renderer still trims/clips at its own platform-appropriate length (Discord clips at 1000 in the container slot, etc.). 4000 is the SDK ceiling, not the per-channel ceiling.
- Risk: A plugin author misuses `longDescription` to bypass the deliberate 256-char `description` discipline on SMS-class channels.
  - Mitigation: `description` is still required by the schema, and SMS-class channels keep reading `description`. The helper makes the contract explicit: long-form for HTML-capable, short-form for everyone else.

target.title: Add CLI tool plan execution
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a deterministic tool-plan execution CLI that replays planned tool calls through the existing Gateway `tools.invoke` boundary instead of creating a parallel dispatcher.

- adds `executeToolPlan` with array and `{ steps }` plan support
- adds `openclaw tools execute-plan [file]` with JSON output, session/agent targeting, approval reporting, and stop-on-block defaults
- wires the new `tools` command into lazy CLI registration and command descriptors
- documents the new CLI page and navigation entry

## Why

This gives integrations and tests a stable way to take model-selected tool steps and dispatch them through OpenClaw's native tool invocation path, preserving normal tool resolution, approval, policy, and `before_tool_call` hook behavior before any tool body runs.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw tools execute-plan` can replay a JSON tool plan through the existing Gateway `tools.invoke` boundary, including plugin-owned tools and `before_tool_call` blocking before the tool body runs.

Real environment tested: Windows 11, Node v24.15.0, OpenClaw source branch `codex/execute-plan-cli` at commit `8e0ecd4275`, foreground Gateway from `openclaw gateway run` on `ws://127.0.0.1:18893`, token auth, one temporary native OpenClaw plugin loaded from `plugins.load.paths`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```powershell
node --import tsx src\entry.ts --profile pr-proof tools execute-plan .artifacts\execute-plan-plugin-proof-plan.json --url ws://127.0.0.1:18893 --token local-proof-token --json --timeout 120000
```

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from the command above:
```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "stopped": true,
  "stopReason": "blocked_tool",
  "steps": [
    {
      "index": 0,
      "action": "pr_allowed_tool",
      "status": "completed",
      "durationMs": 5990,
      "source": "plugin",
      "output": {
        "content": [
          {
            "type": "text",
            "text": "allowed tool body executed"
          }
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "index": 1,
      "action": "pr_blocked_tool",
      "status": "blocked",
      "durationMs": 4141,
      "error": {
        "code": "forbidden",
        "message": "PR proof blocked before tool body"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

Plugin-side execution log from the same run:
```text
2026-05-15T20:33:12.191Z EXECUTE pr_allowed_tool input=before-block
2026-05-15T20:33:16.354Z BLOCK pr_blocked_tool input=must-not-run
```

Observed result after fix: The new CLI executed the first plugin tool through native Gateway `tools.invoke`, then the plugin `before_tool_call` hook blocked the second planned tool with `forbidden`. The blocked tool body did not log an `EXECUTE pr_blocked_tool` line, and the third planned `pr_allowed_tool` step after the blocked call did not run, proving execution stopped at the native tool boundary.

What was not tested: This proof uses a temporary local plugin rather than a published plugin package. The branch still includes unit coverage for aliases, malformed-plan fail-closed behavior, malformed argument fail-closed behavior before invocation, stop-on-block, stop-on-failure, and `--continue-on-error`.

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts src/tools/execute-plan.test.ts`
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/tools-cli.test.ts src/tools/execute-plan.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/cli/tools.md docs/cli/index.md docs/docs.json src/tools/execute-plan.ts src/tools/execute-plan.test.ts src/cli/tools-cli.ts src/tools/index.ts src/cli/program/register.subclis-core.ts src/cli/program/subcli-descriptors.ts`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxlint src/tools/execute-plan.ts src/tools/execute-plan.test.ts src/cli/tools-cli.ts src/tools/index.ts src/cli/program/register.subclis-core.ts src/cli/program/subcli-descriptors.ts`
- `node --import tsx src\entry.ts --profile pr-proof tools execute-plan .artifacts\execute-plan-session-status.json --url ws://127.0.0.1:18891 --token local-proof-token --json --timeout 30000`

target.title: feat(agents): add opt-in identityLine + identityMode config knobs
target.body:
## Summary

OpenClaw hard-codes the system-prompt opening line to:
> "You are a personal assistant running inside OpenClaw."

There's an existing `systemPromptOverride` knob, but it requires authoring the entire prompt from scratch — losing all tooling/skills/memory plumbing. There was no narrow knob for just the identity line.

This PR adds a backward-compatible opt-in:

```jsonc
agents.defaults.identityMode: "default" | "none" | "custom"  // default unchanged
agents.defaults.identityLine: "..."                              // emitted verbatim when mode=custom
```

Per-agent `agents.list[].identityLine` / `identityMode` override defaults.

## Modes

- **`default`** (implicit) — emit the existing assistant string. **Zero behavior change for existing operators.**
- **`custom`** — emit `identityLine` verbatim. Empty/missing line falls back to default.
- **`none`** — suppress the opening identity line entirely. `Current model identity: ...` line and all other plumbing remain.

## Surfaces patched

Production code paths emitting the assistant line (tests + tagline cosmetics excluded):

1. `src/agents/system-prompt.ts:913` — `promptMode === "none"` branch
2. `src/agents/system-prompt.ts:971` — main `stablePrefix` first line
3. `src/cli/capability-cli.ts:96` — uses `DEFAULT_IDENTITY_LINE` constant import (config-resolver isn't wired to that flow)

## Implementation

- New: `src/agents/identity-line.ts` — `resolveIdentityLine()` resolver + `DEFAULT_IDENTITY_LINE` constant + `IdentityMode` type. Per-agent → defaults → fallback resolution, mirroring the `systemPromptOverride` pattern.
- New tests: `src/agents/identity-line.test.ts` (10 resolver cases) + 4 new tests in `src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts` covering both prompt-builder branches.
- Zod schemas + types: `zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts`, `zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts`, `types.agent-defaults.ts`, `types.agents.ts`.
- Wiring: `agent-scope-config.ts` (resolved-agent-config), `system-prompt-config.ts` (resolver wired into render config).
- Cache-key includes the identity line so prompt cache stays correct across overrides.

## Backward compat

Default-path behavior is byte-identical to current main for any operator who hasn't opted in. The cache-key change is additive and preserves correctness across overrides.

## Out of scope (intentionally not touched)

- Renaming the existing `systemPromptOverride` knob (keeps existing operator config working).
- Changing the default identity string itself — operator opt-in only.
- TTS summarizer prompt and security-audit trust-model description (different surfaces, not agent identity).
- `subagent-system-prompt.ts` — it builds its own prompt separately via `buildSubagentSystemPrompt()` with its own role framing (`"You are a subagent spawned by..."`). The `identityLine`/`identityMode` knobs apply only to the main agent prompt paths.

---

Filed in response to a long-standing operator pain about hardcoded "personal assistant" framing constraining downstream identity work. Narrow, backward-compatible, opt-in.

🤖 Authored by Claude (opus-4.6) under Cael's WORKORDER, single-commit.

---

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: OpenClaw's main-agent system prompt has a hardcoded opening identity line (`"You are a personal assistant running inside OpenClaw."`). Operators who want a different agent identity must replace the entire prompt via `systemPromptOverride`, losing all tooling/skills/memory plumbing. This PR adds narrow `identityLine`/`identityMode` config knobs so operators can change just that one line without touching anything else.

**Real environment tested**: DGX Spark (NVIDIA Grace Blackwell GB10, ARM64, 128 GB unified memory), Ubuntu 25.10, Node v25.9.0, pnpm v11.1.0. PR head rebased onto current `upstream/main` at `777d289979` in worktree `/tmp/oc-pr82165-lint`. Full `pnpm install` + production code paths exercised via `buildAgentSystemPrompt()` from `src/agents/system-prompt.ts` (the same builder OpenClaw uses to render every main-agent system prompt in production).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Wrote `src/agents/identity-line-demo.test.ts` (throwaway demo, removed after capture) that invokes `buildAgentSystemPrompt({ workspaceDir: "/tmp/demo", ... })` three times with different `identityLine` configs and prints the first two lines of the rendered prompt to stdout. Ran with:

```
pnpm vitest run src/agents/identity-line-demo.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix**: Below is the live console output captured from the actual `buildAgentSystemPrompt` call (the production prompt builder), showing the three operator-visible modes. These are the literal bytes that would be sent to the model at runtime:

```
=== (1) DEFAULT (no config) ===
You are a personal assistant running inside OpenClaw.
(prompt continues with "## Tooling" section)

=== (2) CUSTOM (identityLine="You are Cael, third prince of the dandelion cult.") ===
You are Cael, third prince of the dandelion cult.
(prompt continues with "## Tooling" section)

=== (3) NONE (identityLine=null) ===
(prompt continues with "## Tooling" section)
Available tools are policy-filtered. Names are case-sensitive; call exactly as listed.
```

Captured from real runtime stdout via `pnpm vitest run` against the rebased PR head; the demo file was deleted after capture so the PR diff is unchanged. Note that mode (3) cleanly drops the opening identity line and resumes with the next stable section, which is exactly the suppression behavior the `none` mode is designed to provide.

Supplementary CI-verifiable receipts (not the primary proof — these are listed only for reviewer convenience):

- `pnpm tsgo` — 0 errors on the rebased head
- `pnpm vitest run src/agents/identity-line.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts` — 93/93 passed (10 resolver + 83 prompt cases)
- `pnpm vitest run src/agents/` (full agents directory, 6 files) — 124/124 passed, no regressions
- `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8` on rebased head — 0 errors across all 3 shards (core: 5360 files, extensions: 8547 files, scripts: 433 files)

**Observed result after fix**: All three modes render exactly as designed. The default-path bytes (mode 1) are byte-identical to current `upstream/main` for any operator who has not set the new knobs, confirming backward compatibility. Custom mode (2) replaces the opening line verbatim with the operator-supplied string. None mode (3) cleanly suppresses the opening line, with the prompt resuming at `## Tooling`. The `stablePrefix` cache key now includes `identityLine`, so cached prompts stay correct across operators with different identity settings (no cross-contamination).

**What was not tested**: Live multi-host fleet rollout under operator-configured `identityLine` is not exercised here — that requires `openclaw config patch` on a deployed seat followed by `openclaw gateway restart`, which is a self-restart hazard from inside a running gateway session and would need an out-of-band operator step on a different host. The narrow code paths the knob touches (resolver + the two `buildAgentSystemPrompt` branches + the `capability-cli.ts` constant) are all exercised above via real-bytes prompt rendering.

target.title: cron: allow retries for local model preflight
target.body:
## Summary
- add configurable retries for isolated cron local-model provider preflight
- expose `cron.modelPreflight.timeoutMs`, `maxAttempts`, and `retryDelayMs`
- document the settings for sleeping/cold-starting Ollama, vLLM, SGLang, LM Studio, and other local providers
- keep defaults unchanged: one 2500ms probe and no retry delay
- enforce one shared 55s preflight deadline across the full fallback candidate chain

## Why
Isolated cron jobs run a lightweight preflight before starting an agent turn for local/private model providers. Current upstream can advance from an unreachable local primary to configured fallback model candidates, but each local candidate still gets only one default 2.5s preflight probe.

That still leaves the cold-start/sleeping-provider case: a local endpoint can become healthy moments after the single probe fails. This PR keeps the fallback behavior intact while letting operators configure a short wake-up window before cron advances to a fallback or marks the run skipped.

The configured per-endpoint retry window is validated at or below 55s, and the runtime now shares one 55s deadline across the complete preflight candidate chain so multi-local fallback paths stay below cron's isolated-agent setup watchdog.

## Review fix
After ClawSweeper noted that the original 55s cap applied per endpoint, this branch now:

- computes one preflight deadline before walking candidates
- passes that same deadline to every candidate preflight
- clamps each local probe timeout and retry delay to the remaining shared budget
- skips additional local endpoint probes once the shared budget is exhausted
- still allows remote/cloud fallback candidates to be selected immediately

## Supplemental verification
- `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/model-preflight.runtime.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.model-preflight.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run-fallback-policy.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts` - 113 tests passed across focused config and cron shards
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts` - 55 tests passed, covering the previously failing unrelated CI shard locally
- `pnpm lint --threads=8` - passed
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check docs/cli/cron.md docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md docs/providers/ollama.md src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/schema.labels.ts src/config/types.cron.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/model-preflight.runtime.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/model-preflight.runtime.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.model-preflight.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts` - passed
- `git diff --check` - passed
- `pnpm tsgo:core` - passed

`pnpm check` currently passes all guards except the local `npm-shrinkwrap.json is stale` guard. The ready-for-review CI run on this branch previously passed the shrinkwrap check, so I did not include unrelated generated dependency metadata in this cron patch.

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior addressed:** A sleeping or cold-starting local model provider can miss cron's default single 2.5s preflight probe even though it becomes reachable moments later. Configured retries should let that same local candidate wake before cron advances to a fallback or skips the run.

**Real environment tested:** macOS local OpenClaw source checkout rebased onto upstream `main`, Node v22.22.0, pnpm v11.2.2. A real loopback OpenAI-compatible HTTP endpoint was started after an 800ms delay. The actual `preflightCronModelProvider` runtime used its real network fetch and SSRF-guard path against `http://127.0.0.1:<port>/v1/models`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `pnpm exec tsx -e` live harness that started the local HTTP endpoint after 800ms and invoked `preflightCronModelProvider` twice: first with default settings, then with `{ timeoutMs: 500, maxAttempts: 2, retryDelayMs: 1000 }`.

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live console output from the real delayed endpoint run:

```json
{"label":"default-single-attempt","result":{"status":"unavailable","provider":"delayed","model":"delayed-local-model","baseUrl":"http://127.0.0.1:54323/v1","retryAfterMs":300000,"reason":"Agent cron job uses delayed/delayed-local-model but the local provider endpoint is not reachable at http://127.0.0.1:54323/v1. Skipping this cron run after 1 preflight attempt; OpenClaw will retry the provider preflight on a later scheduled run. Last error: TypeError: fetch failed"},"elapsedMs":159,"serverHits":0}
{"label":"configured-retry-window","result":{"status":"available"},"elapsedMs":1019,"serverHits":1}
```

**Observed result after fix:** With defaults, the endpoint was reported unavailable before it woke, confirming the cold-start issue still exists. With the configured retry window, the first connection failed while the endpoint was offline, cron waited, the endpoint started, and the next probe succeeded with `status: "available"` and one observed `/models` request.

**What was not tested:** A separate real Ollama daemon was not stopped and restarted during this run. The proof used a real delayed local OpenAI-compatible endpoint to exercise the same production preflight runtime and network path deterministically.

target.title: feat(telegram): bind spawned subagents to forum topics
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds an explicit `supportsAutomaticThreadBindingSpawn` channel capability so automatic thread-bound session spawn support is not overloaded onto `defaultTopLevelPlacement`.
- Wires Telegram `subagent_spawning`, `subagent_delivery_target`, and `subagent_ended` hooks so `sessions_spawn(thread:true)` for subagents can create and bind a child forum topic while keeping Telegram's normal top-level binding placement as `current`.
- Makes the spawn capability per-kind (`subagent` vs `acp`) so Telegram advertises subagent forum-topic spawn support without incorrectly exposing ACP child-topic spawn.
- Documents the new capability seam and adds Telegram/core regression coverage for schema exposure, runtime capabilities, hook registration, binding, completion routing, cleanup, and boundary invariants.

## Context

Telegram already had forum-topic primitives and session binding infrastructure, but it lacked the subagent lifecycle hook wiring and an explicit capability to say: "explicit subagent thread spawns can create child topics, while ordinary top-level binding should stay in the current conversation." Discord and Matrix naturally expose automatic thread spawns through `defaultTopLevelPlacement: "child"`; Feishu keeps `current` placement and does not advertise child-thread session spawn. Telegram now follows the narrower model: keep normal Telegram `/focus` and top-level binding behavior current, while allowing explicit `sessions_spawn(thread:true)` for subagents to create a child forum topic when the Telegram adapter can do so.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram `sessions_spawn(thread:true, mode:"session")` from a channel-bound CodeClaw triage session creates and binds a real Telegram forum topic for the spawned subagent session, so follow-up work routes to that topic instead of becoming invisible or falling back to the parent topic.
- Real environment tested: Live `codeclaw-triage` Docker container after installing a custom OpenClaw package built from combined proof branch `codeclaw-proof-82023-82275` (https://github.com/efpiva/openclaw/tree/codeclaw-proof-82023-82275) at `OpenClaw 2026.5.17 (db1c53a)`. The container was connected to the real CodeClaw Telegram forum supergroup and used the real CodeClaw triage cron job.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```bash
  docker exec --user codeclaw codeclaw-triage     HOME=/home/codeclaw openclaw cron run cc373f35-b483-4c7f-9253-2f6c9c94a595       --wait --wait-timeout 15m --timeout 30000
  ```

- Evidence after fix:

  The triage transcript for run `1d6d77dd-c23b-4b02-9831-e990fb11a7fc` shows the initial PR action for private CodeClaw PR #1223 was spawned with `thread:true` and `mode:"session"`:

  ```json
  {
    "type": "toolCall",
    "name": "sessions_spawn",
    "arguments": {
      "agentId": "main",
      "label": "codeclaw-pr:external:microsoft.ghe.com:bic/lobster:1223",
      "mode": "session",
      "context": "isolated",
      "thread": true
    }
  }
  ```

  The tool result accepted the spawn and returned a thread-bound child session:

  ```json
  {
    "toolName": "sessions_spawn",
    "isError": false,
    "details": {
      "status": "accepted",
      "childSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:c89515e3-1a1e-4465-8fe9-8b25e85c7819",
      "runId": "23538111-3540-4645-a66b-cc0568f96de0",
      "mode": "session",
      "note": "thread-bound session stays active after this task; continue in-thread for follow-ups.",
      "modelApplied": true
    }
  }
  ```

  Live Telegram/OpenClaw logs show the forum topic was created and bound to that child session. The Telegram supergroup id is redacted, but the topic id and session key are unchanged:

  ```text
  2026-05-17T15:07:57.282-07:00 telegram: bound conversation <telegram-supergroup-id>:topic:1167 -> agent:main:subagent:c89515e3-1a1e-4465-8fe9-8b25e85c7819 (idle=24h maxAge=disabled)
  2026-05-17T15:07:57.406-07:00 [telegram] update: {"message":{"message_id":1167,"chat":{"title":"CodeClaw Activity","is_forum":true,"type":"supergroup"},"message_thread_id":1167,"forum_topic_created":{"name":"🤖 codeclaw-pr:external:microsoft.ghe.com:bic/lobster:1223"},"is_topic_message":true}}
  2026-05-17T15:07:57.923-07:00 [agent/embedded] embedded run start: runId=23538111-3540-4645-a66b-cc0568f96de0 sessionId=ada5b4d0-65b5-48ff-8c81-76c2ab477ab8 provider=github-copilot model=gpt-5.5 thinking=medium messageChannel=telegram
  2026-05-17T15:07:58.444-07:00 [agent/embedded] [context-diag] pre-prompt: sessionKey=agent:main:subagent:c89515e3-1a1e-4465-8fe9-8b25e85c7819 ... sessionFile=/home/codeclaw/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/ada5b4d0-65b5-48ff-8c81-76c2ab477ab8-topic-1167.jsonl
  ```

  The persisted Telegram binding file contains the same mapping:

  ```json
  {
    "conversationId": "<telegram-supergroup-id>:topic:1167",
    "targetKind": "subagent",
    "targetSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:c89515e3-1a1e-4465-8fe9-8b25e85c7819",
    "agentId": "main",
    "label": "codeclaw-pr:external:microsoft.ghe.com:bic/lobster:1223",
    "metadata": {
      "threadName": "🤖 codeclaw-pr:external:microsoft.ghe.com:bic/lobster:1223"
    }
  }
  ```

  The CodeClaw poller state also persisted the spawned native session key for that PR:

  ```json
  {
    "source": "pr_reviews",
    "id": "microsoft.ghe.com|bic/lobster#1223",
    "lastKind": "initial",
    "nativeSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:c89515e3-1a1e-4465-8fe9-8b25e85c7819"
  }
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The live Telegram Bot API created forum topic `1167`, OpenClaw bound it to the spawned `main` subagent session, the child session ran with `messageChannel=telegram`, and all child/specialist session transcript files for the spawned review used the `topic-1167` suffix. CodeClaw recorded `nativeSessionKey` for future follow-ups.
- What was not tested: Telegram permission-denied and non-forum failure paths in the live container; those remain covered by local mocked tests. The live proof used one real forum supergroup and one real CodeClaw triage run.

## Supplemental validation


Behavior addressed: Telegram subagent `sessions_spawn(thread:true)` now has an explicit per-kind automatic-spawn capability and lifecycle hooks that bind spawned subagents to child forum topics without enabling Telegram ACP child-topic spawns.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout with mocked Telegram forum topic creation and session binding adapters; no live Telegram Bot API/forum supergroup was used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  extensions/telegram/src/subagent-hooks.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/index.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/thread-bindings.test.ts \
  src/channels/plugins/thread-binding-api.test.ts \
  src/channels/thread-bindings-policy.test.ts \
  src/agents/runtime-capabilities.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts \
  src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/boundary-invariants.test.ts
pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md
git diff --check
pnpm build
codex review --uncommitted
```

Evidence after fix: Targeted Vitest proof passed with 12 files and 245 tests; docs MDX check passed; whitespace check passed; `pnpm build` completed including plugin SDK export verification; final Codex CLI review reported no actionable correctness issues.

Observed result after fix: Telegram advertises `thread` for subagent session spawn through the new explicit per-kind capability, returns a routable Telegram delivery origin for created forum topics, routes completion to the bound topic, cleans up Telegram subagent bindings, and does not advertise/recommend ACP thread-bound spawns when ACP support is disabled for Telegram.

What was not tested: Live Telegram Bot API behavior in a real forum supergroup with `can_manage_topics`; non-forum group and permission failure behavior were covered only through local unit paths/mocked failures.

target.title: feat(tools): add text-only profile
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds a text-only tool profile that explicitly denies all tools.
- Surfaces text-only in tool catalog/default profile metadata.
- Allows managed screenwatch frames in local media roots.
- Updates plugin trust audit coverage so text-only is treated as restrictive.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Text-only tool profile should resolve to a no-tool policy and should not make allowlisted extension plugin tools reachable under the plugin trust audit.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS 26.5, Node v24.14.0, production TypeScript imports via tsx, temporary OPENCLAW_HOME and OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR with a real installed extension directory at state/extensions/some-plugin.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```console
$ tmp=$(mktemp -d); mkdir -p "$tmp/state/extensions/some-plugin" "$tmp/home"
$ OPENCLAW_HOME="$tmp/home" HOME="$tmp/home" OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$tmp/state" node --import tsx -e 'import { collectPluginsTrustFindings } from "./src/security/audit-plugins-trust.ts"; import { resolveCoreToolProfilePolicy } from "./src/agents/tool-catalog.ts"; const cfg = { plugins: { allow: ["some-plugin"] }, tools: { profile: "text-only" } }; const findings = await collectPluginsTrustFindings({ cfg, stateDir: process.env.OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ textOnlyPolicy: resolveCoreToolProfilePolicy("text-only"), pluginReachabilityFindings: findings.filter((finding) => finding.checkId === "plugins.tools_reachable_permissive_policy").length, allFindingIds: findings.map((finding) => finding.checkId) }, null, 2));'
```

- Evidence after fix:

```console
{
  "textOnlyPolicy": {
    "deny": [
      "*"
    ]
  },
  "pluginReachabilityFindings": 0,
  "allFindingIds": []
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The real runtime profile resolver returns `deny: ["*"]` for text-only, and the production plugin trust audit returns zero `plugins.tools_reachable_permissive_policy` findings for an allowlisted installed extension under that profile.
- What was not tested: None

## Test Plan
- git diff origin/main...HEAD --check
- targeted secret scan over PR diff
- pnpm exec vitest run src/security/audit-plugins-trust.test.ts src/agents/tool-catalog.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools-agent-config.test.ts --reporter=verbose
- pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.media.config.ts src/media/local-roots.test.ts --reporter=verbose
- pnpm check

target.title: fix(plugin-sdk): expose skills helper surfaces
target.body:
Fixes #81913

## Summary
- re-export workspace skill loaders and frontmatter metadata helpers from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/skills-runtime`
- re-export host env override sanitizer helpers from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/sandbox`
- add focused SDK contract tests for the new public helper surface

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: third-party plugins can import OpenClaw's supported skills registry, SKILL.md frontmatter, and system-run env sanitizer helpers through public plugin SDK subpaths instead of deep-importing internals or reimplementing them.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw package build from PR head `a12f2aa1b8` (rebased onto current `main` `ed4c4afc0f` for the Dependency Guard check; SDK patch unchanged) on macOS. I built the dist entrypoints, generated plugin SDK declarations, ran runtime postbuild shims, then imported the package through the public self-reference specifiers `openclaw/plugin-sdk/skills-runtime` and `openclaw/plugin-sdk/sandbox`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.plugin-sdk.dts.json --declaration true
node --experimental-strip-types scripts/write-plugin-sdk-entry-dts.ts
node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs
node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-exports.mjs
node --input-type=module -e 'const skills = await import("openclaw/plugin-sdk/skills-runtime"); const sandbox = await import("openclaw/plugin-sdk/sandbox"); const frontmatter = skills.parseFrontmatter("---\nname: Demo\nmetadata: '\''{\"openclaw\":{\"skillKey\":\"demo-key\"}}'\''\n---\n"); console.log(JSON.stringify({ skillsRuntime: { hasLoadWorkspaceSkillEntries: typeof skills.loadWorkspaceSkillEntries === "function", hasParseFrontmatter: typeof skills.parseFrontmatter === "function", skillKey: skills.resolveOpenClawMetadata(frontmatter)?.skillKey }, sandbox: { dangerousHome: sandbox.isDangerousHostEnvOverrideVarName("HOME"), sanitized: sandbox.sanitizeSystemRunEnvOverrides({ shellWrapper: true, overrides: { LANG: "en_US.UTF-8", PATH: "/tmp/bin", TERM: "xterm-256color" } }) } }, null, 2));'
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
OK: All 4 required plugin-sdk exports verified.
```

```json
{
  "skillsRuntime": {
    "hasLoadWorkspaceSkillEntries": true,
    "hasParseFrontmatter": true,
    "skillKey": "demo-key"
  },
  "sandbox": {
    "dangerousHome": true,
    "sanitized": {
      "LANG": "en_US.UTF-8",

    }
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: the built package self-reference imports resolve from the public SDK subpaths, the new skills helpers are callable, the frontmatter metadata resolver returns the expected `skillKey`, and the sandbox env sanitizer filters `PATH` while preserving allowed shell-wrapper variables.

What was not tested: I did not install the package into a separate third-party plugin repo; the proof uses the built local package self-reference and the plugin SDK export checker.
